title: chrysanthemum leucanthemum subsp. crassifolium
intro: Chrysanthemum leucanthemum subsp. crassifolium, also known as the field daisy, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the daisy family, characterized by its white or pinkish flowers and coarse, hairy leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral effects. Consuming the plant or using it in herbal preparations may help to alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant properties, as well as providing antioxidant and immune-boosting benefits. Herbal preparations of Chrysanthemum leucanthemum subsp. crassifolium can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to support overall health and well-being.
